helpful: CVSG cut in statpack.md (27.1% grant vs 3.0% baseline) — the single most useful committed base rate for this cell
Provisioned questions-presented.txt and brief-in-opposition.txt — enough to weigh petition vs BIO without fetching PDFs
fedcourts query granted-2020s priors surfaced Hoffmann v. WBI Energy, a same-conference CVSG grant that made the hold inference concrete
Forward-mode unrestricted web retrieval — the SG's public hold recommendation was the decisive signal and lived only on the open web
gaps: statpack.md in this checkout lacks the 'Segment base rate by salience band' table the prompt tells predictors to anchor on

No provisioned text of the SG's CVSG amicus brief; it is on the docket (May 26, 2026) and is the most outcome-relevant filed document for a post-CVSG cell — worth adding to record/documents for cases with a cvsg_date
supremecourt.gov and justice.gov both 403 the engine's WebFetch, so PDF filings and OSG brief pages are unreachable directly; a pipeline-side fetch or MCP passthrough for those hosts would help
No hold/GVR-aware disposition cut in the statpack (CVSG'd cases that were held for a lead case are folded into denied/granted), which understates P(grant incl. GVR) for hold-pattern cells
notes: Prediction hinged on a public forward signal (SG hold recommendation for St. Mary Catholic Parish v. Roy, 25-581, granted 4/20/2026) rather than the provisioned inputs alone; the provisioned snapshot's post-6/25-conference silence corroborated it.
